note: volume boundary window: does the trip at the end of A continue into the start of B?

pages.text:
crossed the R. Fowey at Fowey on the car ferry (pronounced Foy). __Penmarlan__ * * * * * (name may change)

The campsite served as a storage place for over-wintering boats + has been purchased + improved by Fowey Harbour. Basic + clean. Boring trees around campsite shutting out view. There's a china clay factory in the harbour, loading clay from train from Lostwithiel onto large German ship. Lovely old houses around harbour too. Beautiful warm sun in day, v. cold at night.
__Sunday__
   Blue sky, not a hint of rain. At 9.30. we set off for church in the town. We walked down the hill + caught the ferry. I looked for a hotel to stay in with Di. John + Charles looked after Tonie while Simon + Eve went to church. Matins. Friendly, sermon, good, Bible based, on wives submitting to husbands! We met up with the boys again on the quayside + walked back through the town at a higher level to the ferry. We crossed + took a route back to the van over stiles using foot paths through the fields. After a hot lunch we packed up the tent + left. The Town Fowey, we reached by

the Bodinnick ferry, which takes cars + foot passengers to + fro continuously. We set off looking for a beach for Charles. But after much map reading + travel, including Polperro, we gave up + set off for home. We telephoned Peter ahead + asked him to get ready spaghetti for our arrival at 7.35.

Saturday May 3rd. We left at 3 pm, Simon, Eve, Tonic, to go north. __Quarry Bank Mill__ & the __Styal Estate__ are just south of Manchester. In 1785 Samuel Greg founded a water-powered cotton mill & built the village of Styal for his workers. We arrived so late that only the woods (& shop - calico (Calcutt) shirt + apron bought) were open. Lots of water at different levels. Beech trees at the top of the valleys on the ridges.

   On to __Manchester__, supper in a car park, to hear Thomas play in Strauss' 1868-1946 "Metamorphoseus". Very sad. Racki played too & we heard Wei Mi do a solo. (Racki + Lizzie & Katrin stayed at our house last w/end before the Quartet's playing at Stratford). We camped outside Thomas' Victoria Halls of Residence.

Sunday May 4th. (Eve + Tonic characteristically back dogs near [illegible]). Thomas arrived at 9.30. we picked up Owen & set off for __Tatton Park__ The gardens are beautiful, but Simon thought the outer park landscape even more magnificent. We saw a dove tree with white handkerchiefs. The kitchen garden has green houses being restored - we could

see arched bricks of underfloor heating for 83 greenhouses. We walked around a beautiful Japanese garden but were not allowed in. Gorgeous gunnera beside a massive pond, covered in horse manure.

   We drove a short way into __Knutsford__ & walked around. Very + pretty, expensive town, narrow streets, 3 expensive kitchen shops + dress shops, all shut. We had lunch at Casa Pilinca. Delicious. They made a fuss of Tonic tied up in kitchen courtyard. Trains were too infrequent so we drove Thomas & Owen back to Manchester & set off home, having tea at Keele services.

__Friday May 23rd__.
   We left at 5.35. (Simon, Eve & Tonic). The motorway traffic was stationary so we went on back roads to __Malvern__ for the 7. concert. We reached Worester at 6.43... but just made it running into Big School as the orchestra arrived. We were very proud to have a child playing in

every piece of the first time ever: John 'cello & voice & Charles euphonium & trombone. Brass band, chamber music, chamber choir & jazz band. We slept outside No. 7.
__Saturday__
    Charles sat with us at the Priory. John sang in the choir. There was no sermon, only glorious music. We met up with Jane & Harry & Rocky during coffee & the speeches in the Winter Garden. Ahamed Rashid, a Pakistani, gave the prizes & an excellent speech. Harry said his speech reflected the East in that it was passionate & [illegible]. He had been in No. 4 in the 60's, & had written a book about the Taliban & is a journalist. Keen on Tolerance. Afghanistan.
    John went to stay with Jane to revise for GCSEs while the 3 of us have come to __Llanafan Fawr__. There is a church & pub here & lots of sheep. Beautiful, especially the sky. Some rain. The church has a yew behind 2000 years old. The pub is an old cruck building made from

old ships' timbers.
    __Sunday__ The service is only at 3pm on the second Sunday, so we have missed church. We set off late on our walk because better weather has been forecast for the afternoon. We took a picnic & walked up a valley, round hills & back down another, mostly along a road because the footpaths on the map don't seem to work. I fell & coming down a steep hill with the pack. Tonic has been annoying pulling on the lead. The walk was pretty & varied - about 6 miles. Only one car came along the road all day. It's very remote. Amazing birds - swallows catching bugs. Rooks.
    Charles has been spinning the sheep's wool that we collected & tying a raft of oak twigs. We are south of __Elan Valley__ near Builth Wells.
    We had supper in the Red Dragon, a pub over 1,000 years old mentioned by Giraldus. It has been in the same family for 3[illegible]

Monday May 26th Charles + Eve had showers.
	At 10 we left on another walk, set out in a leaflet that the friendly publican gave us. We walked through a beautiful oak wood, then up a horrible terrifically steep hill. We walked over common land + along a river which was lovely. We had lunch + Simon fell asleep on the river bank while Eve slept on a fallen log. We went through a coniferous wood + up a disused road through fields getting extremely muddy. Toni was a bit better at not pulling. We also passed 2 old churches.
	Back at the campsite we played cards + went to the pub for a drink + had chicken for supper in the van. The only thing wrong with this campsite is that it doesn't have showers.

The rain has held off all day + now it's drizzling in the evening.
Tuesday.
	We left at 9.15. It's damp. We reached Jamie's farm with Charles' map reading. Kate is playing golf. Ben is home after nearly 2 years in Australia. He had lunch with Charles + Eve + is good company. Jamie drove us round the apple orchards. Some of the roots have fungus. Simon + Jamie went off to an apple growers meeting. Charles played on computer games + Eve continued reading "the Lunar len."
	After leaving the farm we went to Holloway's garden shop + ordered 2 Lloyd loom chairs. We drove on home.
__June 15th Sunday__
	After the 10.30. we drove down to Malvern, to Reg Farrar's, for a drinks party.

headings: Monday May 26th Charles + Eve had showers.
pages.text:
Monday May 26th Charles + Eve had showers.
	At 10 we left on another walk, set out in a leaflet that the friendly publican gave us. We walked through a beautiful oak wood, then up a horrible terrifically steep hill. We walked over common land + along a river which was lovely. We had lunch + Simon fell asleep on the river bank while Eve slept on a fallen log. We went through a coniferous wood + up a disused road through fields getting extremely muddy. Tonic was a bit better at not pulling. We also passed 2 old churches.
	Back at the campsite we played cards + went to the pub for a drink + had chicken for supper in the van. The only thing wrong with this campsite is that it doesn't have showers.

The rain has held off all day + now it's drizzling in the evening.
Tuesday.
	We left at 9:15. It's damp. We reached Jamie's farm with Charles map reading. Kate is playing golf. Ben is home after nearly 2 years in Australia. He had lunch with Charles + Eve + is good company. Jamie drove us round the apple orchards. Some of the roots have fungus. Simon + Jamie went off to an apple growers meeting. Charles played on computer games + Eve continued reading "the Lunar [?Men]." 
	After leaving the farm we went to Holloway's garden shop + ordered 2 Lloyd loom chairs. We drove on home.
	__June 15th Sunday__
	After the 10.30. we drove down to Malvern, to Reg Farrar's, for a drinks party.

__Tuesday June 17th__

Samon + Eve set off at 4 for Cambridge + parked at William's house, 7 Maddingley Rd. We got dressed up. Friends fetched William + Bryony from Kings College, where they had been getting ready for the May Ball. Bryony looked fabulous in purple.

William had managed to get us first-sitting dinner tickets in the long room at 8 pm. Thanks to his contacts we didn't have to queue for 3 hours, but walked straight in. It's the longest room lit by candlelight in Europe. Candle brackets on wood panelling. Very beautiful.

We had a wonderful evening, good food + wine, kir ice sorbets, Fireworks + it was dry enough to sit

on the grass despite recent rain. There were students hurling themselves around on bouncy castles + other games.

We sat in the courts + listened to a piano recital + saw lots of dancing to strobe lights.

After going to bed at 3 am, we left at 7.45. for the factory and home.

__Friday 1st August__

Simon, Eve, Thomas, John Charles left at 6 for Germany. We called at the factory to [illegible] pack up + drop off 4 lights that Simon + Sue had forgotten to send.

On to __Ashford__ in Kent, to Broad Hembury Campsite *** [?½] (OK for using en route to Tunnel). 10pm

__Saturday__

The day has started off badly. We thought that there was only bread for breakfast, + that we'd have oat flakes + raisins too. But then

we discovered that I'd forgotten the bread. (I'd taken it back to the kitchen for tea) The [?same] oat flakes were old - Charles couldn't work out whether the date was sell by 1998 or 1988 because weevils had eaten through the lettering. The boys' attitude was a bit pathetic. Lovely hot sunshine.
	It was a long drive to Annie's. Thomas bought bread for lunch. We drove through France, Belgium, Holland + into Germany. It was hot + sticky.
	It was lovely arriving at __Bad Honnef__ + being welcomed by Annie, Klaus, Patrick + Julia, + Annie's goddaughter, Adela. We had a salad supper + walked down to the Island in the Rhine where there was a festival going on. We saw an eel boat which has a beautiful curving shape.
	We slept in the house.
__Sunday__
	Klaus took us on a long bike ride south down the Rhine, using 2 ferries. We took detours through the pretty villages. We

rode fast + it was very hot. At __Linz__ we had lunch by the town gate, delicious salmon salad. There were very high flood water marks. Patrick was very polite staying at the back with me when I was cycling slowly. We stopped at Unkel (Willy Brandt's town) on the way for an ice cream. The trip was from 11.30 - 5.30.
	At home we all needed showers. We used Annie's hammock + she has 2 rabbits.
__Monday__
	We left at 9.40. + spent 1½ hours at Birkenstock, the shoe factory, choosing shoes. Then we got on the motorway towards Frankfurt. We found a supermarket to buy bread + other stuff. Annie had generously given us cold meat + potato salad. We drove into a wood + had lunch in the shade. It's very hot.
	In the afternoon we reached __Heimartshausen__ near __Alsfeld__. There's a swimming pool next door. We had supper - good - at the camp restaurant. Lots of Germans, one Dutch, one Danish. People with dogs.
__Tuesday August 5__
	Hot already when we woke up. I enjoyed a cold shower. You have to pay for

hot ones. We set off for __Alsfeld__ where we saw quaint old buildings, market place, old church with wood & stone carvings & balcony. I sketched this wood carving on the corner wall of a building about 1565. Charlie bought stamps for post cards. Thomas bought fish. Simon bought strawberries. John got embarrassed at my German.

[sketch label:] Markplatz Alsfeld

captions: Alsfeld /Hessen Das Hochzeitshaus 1564-1571 at The Market place
pages.text:
Alsfeld /Hessen
Das Hochzeitshaus
1564-1571
at The
Market place

We drove on to __Eisenach__ & passed Luther's house, where he lived with a family when he was studying. We walked up to Bach's house where his family was living when he was born in 1685. For 132 years Bachs played

the organ here. The house is like Cromwell's house at Ely. Low ceilings, wooden, cosy. After we had looked around a woman played a pipe organ used for [?continuo], a clavichord (quick) ~~a harpsichord~~, a spinet (small harpsichord)

+ then an organ, Thomas pulled straps to pump it.
	We drove on to a campsite by a lake 1km south of __Eisenach__ *44. Charles + Eve swam across the lake. Eve did 2 washes but unfortunately one of the machines was only a drier, so it had to be rewashed in the otter machine later. Thomas is doing the shopping. Good round black pudding (sweet? smoked?).
	Simon + Eve slept badly from having eaten too much!
	__Wednesday__ (St. Elisabeth of Hungary + more)
We arrived at __Wartburg Castle__ + waited ages for a guided tour. The speaker spoke in German, while English people were handed notes. It was a very modern castle, 1160s with some underfloor heating + latrines. lovely carved bases to columns. Much restored in [?C9] + parts reminded us of Arts + Crafts + Pre Raphaelites. musical instruments
Luther translated the New Testament here in 10 weeks when banished from empire 1521 ([?King Friedrich] [?the wise] hid him as 'Junker [illegible]', [illegible] [illegible] Duke of Saxony

Luther 1483-1546 was educated in Eisenach & lived at Luther House, lodging with a family while a student. As an Augustinian Monk, he became a professor, then 1517 nailed his objections to church (We cannot earn Grace) to church door at Wittenburg. He married 1525 & died 1546.
 After visiting the castle we had lunch. Thomas wanted mashed potato but got chips with his sausage. Eve had brocolli rossdes[?], John & Charles had steak. Simon had mince & lentil soup.
 John & Thomas looked around the town while Simon & Eve & Charles looked at Luther's house. There was an amazing video showing the gospels up-to-date. A ghostly schmalzy American Prodigal son with a woman singing & cowboys. A good African telling & a cut-out paper silhouette, German, made in England 1970's. Copernicus, Leonardo, Tetlei (sold indulgences)

Erasmus were Luther's contemporaries.
 We returned to campsite, swam, showered & went to restaurant. A very, very hot day again. Snakes 1m. long in lake apparently! Charles enjoyed the [?on] rubber dinghy.

Eisenach Wartburg

We had supper in the camp restaurant. Thomas got his mashed potatoes + was happy. Schitzel - pieces of pork in breadcrumbs. Lots of salad! Mega fancy icecreams with yellow umbrellas.

     There was a loud scream in the night. Thomas was rearranging his sleeping bag + [?picked up] touched a leathery toad. It was only a foot from his head. He thought it was a snake. Simon went out to the tent with a box + caught it + Simon, Thomas + Charles released it into the grass. Thursday

     This campsite has marvellous groups of animals, stone sculptures for children to ride on, dotted around the grounds (in England they'd be damp + mossy) made out of poured concrete. Mostly family groups of 3, chickens, ponies, elephants, tortoises, alligators, seals.

     We drove to __Erfurt__. It was originally founded, very early on, as a secular university. The cathedral + churches were divided up between the Protestants + Catholics.

     We parked on an empty parking lot. There are lots of old bombed spaces still in E. German towns. A man was selling sausages. We brought back pyjjas to eat at the van at lunch time. Charles felt a wasp go up his shorts + quickly took them off. The sausage seller was bemused!

The Candleabra

Cathedral Mariendom
Erfurt
"Wolgram"
1160

     We walked off to look at the cathedral. An enthusiastic English student took us around from Cambridge. He has joined ARC - to do with Christians working together, welcoming + community. There is an amazing bronze candleabra + lots of carvings + paintings. A very old Pietà

that we liked.
        After lunch Thomas, John & Charles went to an Internet cafe while Simon & Eve walked to an art gallery. It was a very hot walk & the gallery was shut. So we found the boys at the Internet cafe & visited another Church by the Cathedral, St. Severin. We then went on to the home of Annie's friends, in Weimar the Pemmeke family, who are allowing us to stay in their house while they are on holiday. We feel very lucky. It's a lovely modern house & garden! Simon & Thomas & John went to the super market.
        Over the front door they have
        20* C + M + B * 03.  The guide at the cathedral said that this is because this year has been designated the year of the 3 Kings, Caspar, Melchior & Balthasar. Both the Wegner & Gemmekes have very plain bedrooms - a wall or 1½ walls covered by an enormous cupboard with louvred doors, not many pictures.
        The kind cleaner, Elizabeth Lemann, let us in & is coming back early on Saturday to say

captions: Weimar Bauhaus Museum Director's Room Gropius 1924
pages.text:
goodbye & collect the key.
        __Friday August 8th__
        The terrace of the house is lovely to sit on, but we have to eat meals indoors because of the wasps.
        Weimar
        __Bauhaus__
        __Museum__
        Director's
        Room
        Gropius
        1924
        Thomas has greatly enjoyed using a new GPS navigational system on Peter's old laptop.

[annotation top right:] The children are, 12, Monty, Florian & Isabelle

We walked into town, stopping at __Goethe's Summer House__. It was given to him by Duke Carl Augustus. The house is very like Sho House, wooden, cosy. Goethe was a poet, politician, pressed flowers, collected minerals & stones, loved his garden. (Polymath of Soalton)
   We visited the church of St. Peter & Paul where Lucas Cranach the elder completed a triptyd altarpiece 1553, before he died. Lots of Protestant Symbolism, & portraits of Luther, Gatch & patrons. J.S. Bach played the organ 1708-17. Franz Liszt 1848 was choirmaster too & set up a School of Music.
   We had a delicious sausage lunch in the square, looked at the outside of Schiller's house & saw the Bauhaus museum. The furniture looked quite worn & dingy, but had some beautiful lines- very new at the time, 1919-1930. Eve's dressing table light.
   The boys had mega icecreams in the square. (John & Charles gave me a bit) We walked back to the house. Simon walked to the supermarket. Eve did a wash. Simon got supper ready. It's lovely in the garden

captions: Neue Bachgesellschaft Museum — EINTRITT BACHHAUS 8,00 EURO EISENACH — 00586
Pegasus Bridge from the air showing the first house liberated (now a café) & the position where the 3 gliders crash landed. 00.19 hrs June 6th 1944
pages.text:
Pegasus Bridge from the air showing the first house liberated (now a café) & the position where the 3 gliders crash landed.
00.19 hrs June 6th 1944

*** 
   We drove on to __Caen__, where, after much searching, we found the camp site shut. So we have camped in some trees beside the racecourse. We cycled into Caen & saw the Abbaye aux Dames, which is mostly rebuilt, & the Abbaye aux Hommes, which has a plain west front & elegant towers. There are also good misericords.
   We ate supper at Alcide, in the big square with lots of sail boats. The cycle lanes are good. [?Place] [?Toutonne]. Simon had pitchards! I had salmon [illegible] pâté. Then we had blanchette de veau. Simon had tartine & I had chocolate liègeois ice cream.
   There were race horses being shown off in a courtyard in front of a building. We saw trotters on the beach earlier in the day.

Kirby
B15 3JG
86 Westfield Rd, Edgbaston, Birmingham
August 8th 2003 - June 2005

__2003__
1. Weimar, Buchenwald
   Ludwigstadt, Bamburg
3 Rothenb[u]rg
4 Rot-am-See, Heidelberg
6 Hermeskeil [illegible] Ypres
7 London, Burrough, Nether Wotote
8 Malvern, Barnett Castle, High Force
9 Halt Whistle, Vindolanda,
10 Houseteads, Chestars
11 Heatton, Newcastle
12 Rye Court, Colton Valley. __2004__
13 Upton, Malvern
14 Woodrow, Bromyard, W Malvern
15 Beborough, London, Southwark
   Chipping Campden, Malvern
16 Dinas, Harlech
17 Machynlleth
18 Little Crossingham
   Sutton Coldfield
   Southampton
19 Bilbao
20 Guernica, [?Markina]
   Mutreku
21 Puente de la Reina
   Santa Maria Eunate
   Hoz de Lumbier
22 Logrono, Sanguesa
   Ruesta, Lake [?Yesa]
23 Jaca
24 Aragon, Ainsa
27 Plan
28 Bielsa Tunnel
29 Massat, Bedeilhac
30 [?Mas] Melageon, Arc-les-Thermes
31 Montaillou
32 Vernet, Les Sauterelles
33 St. Martin's Abbey
   Canigou
35 Cuca Abbey, [?Elne]
36 Treve, Carcasson
37 Realment
38 Albi
40 Eymentiers, Futuroscope
41 Le Suisse Normand
42 Normandy Beaches
   Bayeux
43 Lodques, Point du Hoc
   Barfleur
45 Bath
46 Trumpley, Bromyard
   Malvern Court
48 Harbury Hell
__2005__
50 Aston Cantlow
51 Kinwarton
52 Malvern [?Bromyard]
   PTO

Guggenheim Museum
Bilbao
1997 by Frank O'Gehry

53 Knapton
54 Cambridge
   Oxburgh Hall
   Mileham
   East Dereham
56 Reepham
   Blickling Hall
   Halesworth
57 Corby

Saturday August 6 2005
At 9.15. we left the Gemmeke's house in Weimar. We are: Thomas 23, John about to be 16, Charles 14 + Simon & Eve.
   We drove to Buchenwald, arriving at 9.5[illegible]. __Buchenwald__ was the prototype concentration camp + had satelite camps. It was where the SS trained. 1937-45. Then the Russians ran it for 5 years.
   250,000 passed through. 50,000 died, of [?whom] 1,000 were strangled. Many were shot in the back of the neck. They were "Social misfits", politicians, Jehovas Witnesses, Jews, homosexuals. The week they were liberated, April 11th 1945, the American soldiers made local Weimar people walk through the camp. We saw the ovens.
   It's very hot. We stopped at a supermarket, then had lunch next to a railway line. We drove on to find a campsite by a lake. The first one was full. We've stopped at __Neumannshof__. It's crowded, no shade, + the swimming is a bit dirty-looking down a steep shale slope. John + Eve had trouble with the credit-card showers. Lots of instant bbqs stink. There's a barking dog right next to us.
Sunday
We left to drive Thomas to the station at Saalfeld for an 11.45 train. We discussed how poor E. Germa[ny illegible]

feale. As we drove through, there is much ugliness, few little leasure facilities (tennis courts), little beauty. Broken-down factory units, empty bombed lots. One of the few signs of prosperity are expensive cars. Thomas was annoyed that there were no shops at the station. Simon remarked that the time-span since the Wall came down – 1991-2003 – is the same as for our childhood since WWII 1946-58. And the country/town regeneration is probably similar to Britain then.

    It's still hot & sweaty all the time.
    Steinbach/Heide we have just passed one of the prettiest villages I have ever seen. It's in a wooded valley. At the top of the centre of the village is a pond. The whole of the village green was allotments, full of flowers, at the bottom was a church.
    We saw a swimming pool from the road. It was large & inviting & not very full. Ludwigstadt. Beautifully clean & quiet. We swam, had lunch in the van in the carpark & swam again. Charles spent ages on the 3.5m. (11ft) diving board.
    Things in the East seem to be organised for the workers. The customer/consumer doesn't matter. When there is a road block for road works, no 'deviation' signs are given. Customers have to manage as best they can. ****
    We drove on to Bamburg. We are back in

West Germany now. The campsite owner spoke English & told us we could park wherever we like on the site. There is a river. For the first time, the showers are free. All through Germany the washrooms have been spotless.
    We have been eating sausage, salami, black pudding & fruit – peaches & grapes. We bought a packet of sauerkraut which tasted horrible. The Top is good.
    The fridge has packed up. It has been the hottest holiday ever, & sometimes we have left the gas fridge on when going along & it should have been on electric only.
    This campsite is nice & dark at night. Some of them have been terribly bright.

    Monday August 11"
    This is the nicest campsite we've been in. We caught the bus into Bamburg & looked at the cathedral. There are lots of sculptures, old & new. "The horse rider" is (13). In a small crypt is a beautiful new sculpture on an old theme – a bishop presenting a cathedral. It is sort of cubist, with his eye brow, nostrils & mouth slightly asymmetrical. There are also beautiful panels in the altar & lecturn. There is also an enormous crypt that was [illegible]

[Photo of equestrian statue, caption top-right: The Horse Rider Bamburg Cathedral (3.)]

We ate at a rather grubby pub. Walking round the town was pleasant but hot. There was a memorial to Graf von Stauffenberg in an [?arch] [marginal annotation: July 23rd '44] [marginal annotation: Old Rathau[s]] where 2 women played chambermusic in the building on the island on the bridge over the river. Bamburg has many buildings that are

original, it was not much bombed by the British.

Back in the campsite Simon is fiddling with the fridge. The boys have fallen back on the usual absorbtion: playing with ants. The ants found Charles wasp trap + they have set up the camera on the tripod to photograph it. I am lying on the blue mat under a magnificent oak Tree. Charles rowed + Eve swam upstream + back.

We had supper in the restaurant - the boys had spaghetti + Simon + I had delicious meat + veg stew. After supper we chatted. There are Chinese campers here.

__Tuesday__ We woke at 7.40.

We left at 9.30. The previous evening we watched a man + his wife erecting a collapsible towed caravan (35 min). They left just after we got up. We drove south to Rothenburg. It's an amazing town, built [?at] a very steep hill above a wriggly river. It was rich until [illegible] + then became a backwater because it was Protestant, so it was never modernised. [It] made preservation orders everywhere. It has lovely expensive shops, icecreams + cake shops. We went into St. Jacobs church, named after St. James of Compostella. The altarpiece is again carved by Riemenschneider. I promised John

captions: Altar of The Holy Blood in St. Jacob's church Rotherburg ob der Tauber by Tilman Riemen schneider 1499 1504
pages.text:
& Charles icecreams if they would sit & look at it. There's a relic in the middle of the cross. St. Judas is in the middle being handed a piece of bread. Beautifully carved & pleasing composition. His of figures often seem to hold pieces of cloth.

[photograph of the Altar of the Holy Blood]

Altar of The Holy Blood in St. Jacob's church Rotherburg ob der Tauber by Tilman Riemen schneider 1499 1504

After that we went to the torture museum, which got a bit monotonous. It went on until [18?]. We walked along paths below the town. There are still huge gates & town walks, a beautiful town, but totally touristified. [Even McDonalds has] [illegible] [illegible] bought cakes & walked back to the van. It's still hot.
   We have driven on to Rot-am-see [****] [(Ettlingen?)] Although it mentions lake, we are not allowed to swim in it. So we are having a quiet evening. The campsite feels like a ghost-town, people must only come at weekends. (A car of young people has dropped off 2 tents & driven off - LT - Lithuania?)
   This is the first campsite where Charles has been able to eat with us at the table, instead of hiding in the loo, because there was only one wasp (spheksophobia - fear of wasps) at each meal. All the rubbish in the litter bins was contained under lids, whereas other campsites had disgraceful stench piles overflowing. Perhaps the families & old fogeys at the [wend?] won't tolerate dirt, unlike holiday campers. There's no swimming at the camp & [there's] not much to do, but it's pleasant. The old man running the camp switched the light

captions: Heidel berg Castle (ruins) behind the old Bridge
pages.text:
of when I was having a shower. Simon switched the light off when I was on the loo. Such energy conservation! Infact, this is the first campsite with dirt in loo showers, left by lithuanians. We have been ~~dec~~ clearing up after them.
__Wednesday__ We are off to __Heidelberg__ to pick up Thomas who has been staying with Ingvo in Lichtenstein. In the town we parked near a super market & shopped (only dry Salami & tinned herring because no fridge) then walked to the station to meet Thomas. It took ages to ~~watt~~ find a parking space.

[photo: Heidelberg Castle (ruins) behind the Old Bridge]

Heidel berg Castle (ruins) behind the old Bridge
We parked near the castle & caught the funicular down the hill. It was boring - it went in a tunnel all the way. We had lunch at an Italian restaurant. We looked at the Heiliggeistkirche church & saw King Rupert, Ruprecht's gravestone with his wife Elizabeth von Hohenzollam from 1706-1936 it had a dividing wall so that one end was protestant & the other catholic. We trained back

up to the castle & saw the gardens, the apothecary museum & the Grosses Fass, Big Barrel, it was made for Prince Karl Theodore in 1751 from 130 oak tree trunks. It held 221,725 litres. It's 8.5 m across & 7m high with a dance floor on top. The people at the castle used to drink nearly 2,000 litres a day.
We lacked ~~energy~~ energy because of the boat. We reached the Haide Campsite, 5k. along the north side of the River Neckar from Heidelberg.
I again had trouble with the showers. Having undressed & hung my clothes on the hooks, I had to rehang them to open the door to put the token in the metre. Once I'd done this, water started pouring out of the next-door shower...
We needed clothes, so I did a wash & Simon got annoyed at having to hang a washing line after supper. In the middle of the night the sky looked cloudy. So I took in half the washing. Later it started to rain, so I brought in the rest. It has drizzled this

morning. Fortunately nearly all the clothes are dry (except 2 boxers) & we've been able to put them away. We were parked next to a porter from Clifton.

__Thursday__
   We are leaving the campsite at 9.40.. There's free internet access. The pitch was quite noisy with (working?) boats going up + down the river + trains just the other side of the river. Further back, there would have been more space in the campsite. We could order bread for breakfast. It was quite a good campsite.
   There are 2 sets of double locks on the river.

   We drove + drove westward with Charles map-reading. Thomas is back, doing more Rebel Personality GPS navigating on the laptop. He got very shirty at the rud. Thomas + John were both annoyed that Simon + Eve walked across the field to the chapel. It was a graveyard. The war monument was sad – one name in WWI + 3 names in WWII were the same. There was an odd, uncommon name – Leo Junior.

   Next to our lunch field were planes, it turned out to be an aircraft museum, founded 1973 by Leo Junior. Amazing place. V. enjoyable. Sorry Peter's not here. A rocket, helicopters, passengers, transport, planes, fighters, bomber Fokkers, Heineke, Hornier, British, [?MiG], US, French, Russian, Engines, auto-giro, ejecto-seat

Orville mentions, Bleriot plane + Demoiselle, a go-cart with wooden pedals + a lawn mower engine suspended over it 1909. They've made a little cafe in the Concorde. Leo's son, Richard, seems to be involved too.

[GLUED TICKET: FLUGAUSSTELLUNG / I. & P. Junior / Tel. 06503 - 7693 Fax 3410 / 54411 HERMESKEIL 2 / 151 / 23 –]

   We arrived at [?Le Val d'Enverande] campsite at Neufchateau + drove into the village for supper. Good tomato soup. The campsite is very basic. Not only no-soup soap, no loo paper, but also no loo seats either, but it clean. People had dogs fenced in round their caravans. A nice meadow nearby with cows. The campsite owner had chickens.

Friday
   We're in a pretty area, I've seen 3 country houses. The area is described here –

[GLUED TOURIST BROCHURE TEXT: Friends visitors, by choosing our area for your stay, you resolutely chose to breath. With the doors of this green lung that is the forest of Anlier to the crossroads of the motorways, in the heart of nature, central Arden is a true space of relaxation, of adventure, of leisure, of sport, excursions and rest. Our area abounds in ideas of stays or escapades: the peaceful nature, forest, rivers, villages, the architectural heritage, the quality and variety of lodging, good tables, succulent soils products, walks on foot, horse, bicycle. In any season tourist, cultural and sport festivities. Thus profit from the convivial manner of the inhabitants and the heat of their reception. With this first edition of the tourist brochure, the "Syndicat d'Initiative du Pays de Neufchateau" hopes to make even more pleasant your stay in central Arden. Be welcome!]

   We've run out of bread + are using up the left-over bread, but the boys were decisive about the quality of bread [here / rest cut off]

We stopped at a Spar supermarket. But forgot to buy tea.... After lunch on a road off the motorway we reached Ypres + bought chocolate. Then we visited the WW1 Museum. It was very sad. The buildings of Ypres + the trees around were all flattened. They found the remains of a soldier when Charles visited in the spring. The sound effects + poetry reading were good.

    We had supper on the Square + walked down to hear the last post at the Menin Gate. It was very crowded. 2 bugles played. An old man from Yorkshire laid a wreath. 3 teenagers also laid a wreath.
    We drove on to a campsite __Kenmels__[?Kenmelst] where we stayed before when we came to Belgium with all the boys to see a show for Chad. It's on a ridge that was fought over near the end of WW I.

    Charles' chocolate label on the box.
    We wondered, over supper, whether German + English use alliteration in advertising slogans more than the Romance languages do.

[glued-in business card: 't Heimelryck / Zoetheden & Streekspecialiteiten / MEENSESTRAAT 10 • 8900 IEPER / TEL: 057/360.361]

Saturday August 16th
    The campsite had a beautiful view out over the ridge. We left at 9.10., 20 min early!

We just missed getting on the earlier [train] by one car. Our own train was taken off because it was late arriving from England, + our actual train left at 10.43, which was very annoying. [annotation: (10.34)]
    We dropped off Thomas + John at Ashford station to go + visit William in his new house at [?Burrough] in London. There were big jams on the M25. We picked up Tonic from Kennels on the way home.

__Saturday August 23rd.__
    Simon, John, Charles, Eve + Helen set off to go to the Cotswolds to camp with David, Jean + [?Dylan] (Sally's in Istanbul + [?Maria's] in France) who are bringing William + Bryony from London, + Tim + Margaret. We stopped at Beckett's Farm for food.
    We reached __Nether Westcote__ at 12.45. It's a village, one of several, on the row of springs half way up the line of hills. Tim + Margaret arrived at 13. John has just texted William with his new mobile phone to find out when they are arriving.
    When they arrived we all had tea. There is a lovely old lane next to the campsite where I walked Tonic. David cooked supper, or organised the

cooking of supper. He cooked sausages, tomatoes & potatoes. He gave Margaret French beans to cook & me [struck:bread] broccoli. Simon & I walked up the road & looked at the distant glow of town lights (Oxford, Cheltenham?). It rained in the night.

    Ag __Sunday__
        Aylan ate with us & Jem & David with Grandma. At 11 we left the campsite carrying a picnic. William & John were wearing sandals with no heel straps. Grandma also wore sandals because she forgot to change. It was quite warm. We walked down the old road at the side of the site which led down into the valley. At the bottom there were difficult ploughed fields that we found it difficult to walk through. The Oxfordshire Way had been ploughed up. We went through Sledington & Simon found a marvellous place for a picnic under a huge shady tree, by the R. Evenlode. We walked back out of the valley by road passing through Idbury. In the evening we had supper at the New Inn, our campsite pub.

    Monday
        We chatted & [?of] had coffee & left at 11.30., bringing Aylan back to Birmingham

Sunday October 19th
    S+E  We left at 5 to pick up Jim, Margaret & Helen to hear John play his 'cello in an informal concert. He played very well, one of his grade 8 pieces, & in a 'cello quartet. The best we'd heard him play for ages.
__Friday October 24th__
    We should have left at 4.30., but 10 minutes before getting in the van I saw Tonic had a sore eye. So I dashed to the vet, got stuff for conjunctivitis, & dashed back. [marginal annotation: I put the ointment in his wrong eye first] We left at 5.10... It was a long, long drive north. We are camping at Doe Park __Cotherstone__, NW Barnard Castle.
    There is a dog walk, lovely views, old Georgian house & HEATED toilets. This is important because we are all absolutely __freezing__. I am wearing a sweater, an arran cardigan, a sheepskin waistcoat & a jacket & my hands are nearly too cold to write. I wish I'd brought boots. The van heater is broken.

captions: High Force, the highest waterfall in Britain, only had water in this ↓ channel.
pages.text:
__Saturday__
	It's a fine morning. The boys showered because there may be no showers this evening.
	We drove to High Force. There were

[photo of waterfall]

High Force, the highest waterfall in Britain, only had water in this ↓ channel.

canoeists in the river. It's in Teesdale. We very much like the countryside.
	We drove on to __Haltwhistle__, had lunch & met Peter (from Newcastle Uni) off the train. Then we went on to Vindolanda. Rain threatened, but apart from walking back to the car (when we saw a glorious [?&] double rainbow) we were lucky.
	There have been an incredible number of finds, every year. There are still 30,000 unexcavated graves. The conditions have preserved stuff exceptionally well — wooden tablets with writing on, leather shoes with delicate cut-out leather uppers, coins, hair combs & a horse's leather helmet.
	They have a reconstructed turf wall, wooden [?] fort & typical stone tower. A good museum.
	The campsite is at Bardon Mill. [?] it is a very pretty field next to the R. Tyne, lots of rabbit holes for Tonic to search in. Quite sheltered, so less windy than last night, but a heavy frost. Cold again, but not quite so

captions: The latrines at Housesteads, as they were
pages.text:
cold. The van heater is broken. We had a good supper in the local pub, with a roaring fire.

    We asked how much it cost at the campsite & the farmer said £3 a night & John thought he said "£3 and 8".

[photo: The latrines at Housesteads, as they were] [caption written vertically along right edge: The latrines at Housesteads, as they were]

[text continues bottom-right, partially cut off or continued on next spread]

[small partial text visible at top-left gutter from left page: ...ra / the / [illegible]]

[postcard/illustration: artist's reconstruction of Romans using the latrines at Housesteads]

    The sun shone in the morning. Toni got filthy digging his [?own] way into rabbit holes.

    We went to __Housesteads__, I remembered the loos.
On to __Chesters__, where there is a Roman Bridge abuttment. Hadrian's wall is 73 miles long (90 Roman miles) We had lunch in the carpark + walked around in good weather.

pages.sketch_desc: A hand-drawn floor plan sketch of Peter's house, showing a rectangular outline with internal partitions suggesting rooms. Lines and boxes appear to indicate doors and rooms. The sketch accompanies text describing the house layout (3 bedrooms, living, kitchen & bathroom).
pages.text:
In the evening we reached __Newcastle__. Simon & Eve went to church at St. Gabriel's. Peter lives at 6 St. John's Terrace, Haxton, which is ENE of the city centre, behind Sykes' Wall, which is visible from the bridge.
	We had supper before Evensong + then left straight away for the movie which was a looooong 30 minute walk away. We saw "Finding Nemo."
	Monday
	John & Charles sleep on Peter's floor, they spend alot of time in playing Kieran's James Bond computer game. Tord is the other housemate who organises things.
	Peter's house, which looks 4 ft. [illegible] but is, infact, up a flight of stairs to 3 bedrooms, living, kitchen & bathroom
	Simon & Eve slept in the van. It was much warmer in town + more comfortable + Peter has central heating. We saw where P[eter] [illegible] News. St. Nicholas Cathed[ral] Peter had a lesson at 5pm. He was

with us all day. We went to the Baltic Flour Mill & saw lots of exhibitions - woodwork, slide shows (dislocated), had lunch in a fish place, then we went shopping & to the Laing Gallery; this was mostly closed for refurbishing, but there was a good exhibition on arts of NE - glass, pottery, Bewick engraver, cloth (acetate, nylon, acrylic.)
    There was an old jug, white & pink
"Have communion with few
  Be familiar with one
  Deal justly with all
  Speake evil of none."
    We returned to the house for Peter to set off for his class. I walked Toni in Heaton Park. He seemed quite happy sitting in the car.
    We ate at the same Bistro restaurant. Arlequin CCX

Tuesday October 28th
    Simon & Eve went into Peter's flat again for breakfast.
    Eve walked the dog in the park.
    We went to the Hatton Gallery, the university museum, & saw a marvellous exhibition of Kenneth Rowntree's paintings 1915-97, a Quaker & a Yorkshireman.
    We stopped & had a good, cheap lunch. We caught the Metro back to Peter's house, buying Peter a crash-helmet for cycling to the university. He had an afternoon class & we set off home through Newcastle traffic jams over one of the bridges. We left at 2pm & stopped in York for a cracked light. There were 2 more jams on the motorway & we only got home at 10pm.
